'''Daryl Millard''' (born 20 February 1985) is a [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. He previously played for the [[Catalans Dragons]], [[Bulldogs Rugby League Football Club|Canterbury-Bankstown]] and [[St George Illawarra Dragons|St George Illawarra]].

==Background==
Millard was born in [[Kogarah, New South Wales, Australia]] and is Australian-Fijian descent.

His father Shane Millard is a former reserve-grade coach for the [[St George Dragons]],<ref name="Brown">{{cite news|last=Brown|first=Alex|newspaper=The Sydney Morning Herald|url=http://www.smh.com.au/news/league/dragons-ball-boy-now-carrying-his-sides-hopes/2006/09/11/1157826876845.html|title=Dragons ball boy now carrying his side's hopes|date=12 September 2006|accessdate=27 August 2009}}</ref> and his younger brother [[Ryan Millard]] plays for the [[Shellharbour City Dragons]] in the [[New South Wales Cup]] and is also a [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] international.<ref name="Millard eyes Bati">{{cite web|url=http://www.fijitimes.com/story.aspx?id=136601|title=Millard eyes Bati|work=Fiji Times|date=3 January 2010|accessdate=6 April 2010|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120825200425/http://www.fijitimes.com/story.aspx?id=136601|archive-date=25 August 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Millard was a Renown United junior.<ref name="Brown"/> Millard made his first grade début for the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] in 2006, scoring four tries in the season.<ref> [https://web.archive.org/web/20061208052812/http://www.dragons.com.au/locker_room/player_profiles/Daryl_Millard.asp]</ref> He has played centre, wing and fullback in Jersey Flegg and Premier League and has established himself as a centre in first grade. He joined Canterbury-Bankstown in 2007.

Millard played 25 games for [[2007 NRL season]] and scored 13 tries as Canterbury finished sixth on the table.  Millard played in both finals games for the club which both ended in defeat.  In the [[2008 NRL season]], Millard played 12 games as Canterbury finished last on the table and claimed the wooden spoon.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://the81stminute.com/2017/08/most-boring-team-ever-2008-wooden-spoon-bulldogs-would-beat-2017-version-by-twenty-points/|title=Most boring team ever? 2008 wooden spoon Bulldogs would beat 2017 version by twenty points|website=The81stminute|date=12 August 2017 }}</ref>

In 2009, Millard played 11 games for Canterbury-Bankstown as the club finished second on the table and qualified for the finals.  Millard missed out on playing in the club's finals campaign as they reached the preliminary final but were defeated by arch rivals [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] at [[Telstra Stadium]].

In 2010, Millard joined the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] team in the [[Super League]] competition on a 2-year contract. He effectively replaced [[Ryan Atkins]] who joined the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=34452 |title=R League |access-date=7 September 2009 |archive-date=3 January 2010 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100103125247/http://rleague.com/db/article.php?id=34452 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

In June 2012, he signed a new deal with [[Catalans Dragons]], further extending his stay at the club till end of 2014 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18634740|title=Daryl Millard|publisher=BBC Sport |access-date=30 June 2012}}</ref>

In Winter 2014, Millard signed for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] on a 'Train & Play' basis after completing his contract with the [[Catalans Dragons]].

His younger brother [[Ryan Millard]] signed for the [[Rochdale Hornets]] after impressing in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].

In Round 4 of the [[2015 NRL season]], Millard made his South Sydney début against the [[Parramatta Eels]] as a {{rlp|ce}}<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2015/round-4/parramatta-vs-south-sydney/summary.html |title=NRL 2015 – Round 4 |publisher=Rugby League Project |date=2015-03-27 |accessdate=2022-05-19}}</ref> and played there until round 7 whilst [[Dylan Walker]] was out with an injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTItMTY2MC00LS0tLS0tMzUyMi0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0= |title=Custom Match List |publisher=Rugby League Project |date=2020-01-01 |accessdate=2022-05-19}}</ref>

==International career==
Millard was named in the [[Fiji national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Training Squad|Fiji training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Bati pencil in new Newcastle pace ace Uate">{{cite web|url=http://www.fijitimes.com/story.aspx?id=96697|title=Bati pencil in new Newcastle pace ace Uate|publisher=Fiji Times Online|date=1 August 2008|accessdate=8 August 2008|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120322005015/http://www.fijitimes.com/story.aspx?id=96697|archive-date=22 March 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Fijian preliminary squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/08/06/fijian-preliminary-squad.aspx|title=Fijian preliminary squad|publisher=RLWC08.com|date=6 August 2008|accessdate=2 August 2008|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080810112612/http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/08/06/fijian-preliminary-squad.aspx|archivedate=10 August 2008}}</ref> He was named in the [[Fiji national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Training Squad|Fiji]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm|title=Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads|publisher=BBC|date=8 October 2008|access-date=10 October 2008}}</ref>

He was named in the Fiji squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://fijilive.com/sports/news/2013/09/civoniceva-wes-naiqama-to-lead-fiji-bati/32752.Fijilive|title=Civoniceva, Wes Naiqama to lead Fiji Bati|accessdate=2015-12-26|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304054057/http://fijilive.com/sports/news/2013/09/civoniceva-wes-naiqama-to-lead-fiji-bati/32752.Fijilive|archivedate=4 March 2016}}</ref> Millard and his brother were selected for the Fiji Bati squad for the [[International rugby league in 2015#Melanesian Cup|2015 Melanesian Cup]] test-match. Both played for the Fiji in the first test match against the PNG Kumuls on 2 May 2015 at Cbus Super Stadium, Robina, Gold Coast, Australia.<ref>{{cite web|author=Chris Kennedy|url=http://www.nrl.com/fiji-v-papua-new-guinea-five-key-points/tabid/10874/newsid/85894/default.aspx |title=Fiji v Papua New Guinea: Five key points |publisher=National Rugby League |date=2015-05-02 |accessdate=2022-05-19}}</ref>
